NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

The script reports information on how the hostname of the target
  was determined.
This plugin performs service detection.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

This script tries to detect / enumerate different HTTP server
  banner (e.g. from a frontend, backend or proxy server) by sending various different HTTP requests
  (valid and invalid ones).

Collect information about the network route and
  network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Enumerates which HTTP methods are allowed.
Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.

This script detects and reports the HTTP Server's banner
  which might provide the type and version of it.

  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server mod_session checks the session expiry time before
  decoding the session. This causes session expiry time to be ignored for mod_session_cookie sessions since the
  expiry time is loaded when the session is decoded.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server, a race condition in mod_auth_digest when running in a

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

  scoreboard. Non-Unix systems are not affected.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server mod_session checks the session expiry time before
  decoding the session. This causes session expiry time to be ignored for mod_session_cookie sessions since the
  expiry time is loaded when the session is decoded.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

  configured access control restrictions.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
By sending request bodies in a slow loris way to plain resources, the h2
  stream for that request unnecessarily occupied a server thread cleaning up that incoming data. This affects only
  HTTP/2 connections. A possible mitigation is to not enable the h2 protocol.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes
  ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while
  other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_http2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Using fuzzed network input, the http/2 request handling could be made to
  access freed memory in string comparison when determining the method of a request and thus process the request
  incorrectly.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

Consolidation of PHP detections.

This script consolidates the OS information detected by several
  VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.

  Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It
  also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.

  If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the
  referenced community forum.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

All known security headers are being checked on the remote web
  server.

  On completion a report will hand back whether a specific security header has been implemented
  (including its value and if it is deprecated) or is missing on the target.

The script reports information on how the hostname of the target
  was determined.

This script detects and reports the HTTP Server's banner
  which might provide the type and version of it.

  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  forum.

  Reconnaissance & Exfiltration via Adaptive Compression of Hypertext) attack.

  instance affecting a specific piece of software. To be vulnerable, a web application must:

  - Be served from a server that uses HTTP-level compression

  - Reflect user-input in HTTP response bodies

  - Reflect a secret (such as a CSRF token) in HTTP response bodies

  - CVE-2018-25138: FLIR AX8 Thermal Camera

  - CVE-2018-25147: Microhard Systems IPn4G

  - CVE-2019-25241: FaceSentry Access Control System

  - CVE-2019-25291: INIM Electronics Smartliving SmartLAN/G/SI

  - CVE-2020-29583: Zyxel Firewall / AP Controller

  - CVE-2020-36915: Adtec Digital SignEdje Digital Signage Player

  - CVE-2020-9473: S. Siedle & Soehne SG 150-0 Smart Gateway before 1.2.4

  - CVE-2021-27797: Brocade Fabric OS

  - CVE-2021-47744: Cypress Solutions CTM-200/CTM-ONE

  - CVE-2023-1944: minikube 1.29.0 and probably prior

  - CVE-2023-53983: Anevia Flamingo XL/XS

  - CVE-2024-22902: Vinchin Backup & Recovery

  - CVE-2024-31970: AdTran SRG 834-5 HDC17600021F1 devices (with SmartOS 11.1.1.1) during a window
  of time when the device is being set up

Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a man-in-the-middle attack vulnerability.

Apache HTTP Server through 2.4.23.

Summary:
The OpenSSH client code between 5.4 and 7.1p1 contains experimental support for resuming SSH-connections (roaming).
  The matching server code has never been shipped, but the client code was enabled by default and could be tricked by a malicious
  server into leaking client memory to the server, including private client user keys. The authentication of the server host key prevents exploitation
  by a man-in-the-middle, so this information leak is restricted to connections to malicious or compromised servers.
Affected Software/OS: OpenSSH >= 5.4 < 7.1p2

  VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.

  referenced community forum.

MySQL protocol-based detection of MariaDB / Oracle MySQL.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.
Consolidation of jQuery detections.

The script reports information on how the hostname of the target
  was determined.

This script detects and reports the HTTP Server's banner
  which might provide the type and version of it.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

All known security headers are being checked on the remote web
  server.

  On completion a report will hand back whether a specific security header has been implemented
  (including its value and if it is deprecated) or is missing on the target.

Collect information about the network route and
  network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.

This script tries to detect / enumerate different HTTP server
  banner (e.g. from a frontend, backend or proxy server) by sending various different HTTP requests
  (valid and invalid ones).

This script consolidates the OS information detected by several
  VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.

  Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It
  also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.

  If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the
  referenced community forum.
Consolidation of PHP detections.

The remote host is exposing a 'composer.json' and / or 'composer.lock'
  file of the Composer dependency manager for PHP. This file(s) might expose some information about the
  structure of the web application and the installed software / dependencies on the remote host.

Summary: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.

Collect information about the network route and
  network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
This plugin performs service detection.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

This script consolidates the OS information detected by several
  VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.

  Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It
  also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.

  If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the
  referenced community forum.

The script reports information on how the hostname of the target
  was determined.
MySQL protocol-based detection of MariaDB / Oracle MySQL.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server, with MPM event, worker or prefork, code executing in
  less-privileged child processes or threads (including scripts executed by an in-process scripting interpreter)
  could execute arbitrary code with the privileges of the parent process (usually root) by manipulating the
  scoreboard. Non-Unix systems are not affected.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server mod_session checks the session expiry time before
  decoding the session. This causes session expiry time to be ignored for mod_session_cookie sessions since the
  expiry time is loaded when the session is decoded.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.20 < 2.4.44 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.2 to 2.4.43.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server mod_http2 null pointer dereference DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.25.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server HTTP/2 connection DoS Vulnerability
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS:
Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.18 through
  2.4.30 and 2.4.33.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.17 < 2.4.49 mod_proxy HTTP/2 Request Smuggling Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to an HTTP/2 request smuggling
  vulnerability in the 'mod_proxy' module.
Affected Software/OS:
Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.17 through 2.4.48 running the
  mod_proxy module together with an enabled HTTP/2 protocol.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.20 - 2.4.39 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.20 to 2.4.39.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server, a race condition in mod_auth_digest when running in a
  threaded server could allow a user with valid credentials to authenticate using another username, bypassing
  configured access control restrictions.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.58 mod_macro Out-of-bounds Read Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to an out-of-bounds read
  vulnerability in mod_macro.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.57 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.59 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.58 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server Denial of Service Vulnerability-02 (Apr 2018) - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server Denial-Of-Service Vulnerability (Jun 2017) - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS:
Apache HTTP Server versions 2.2.32, 2.4.24
  and 2.4.25.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to read
  secret data from process memory if the Limit directive can be set in a user's .htaccess
  file, or if httpd.conf has certain misconfigurations, aka Optionsbleed.
Affected Software/OS:
Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x versions up to 2.2.34 and
  2.4.x below 2.4.28.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 < 2.4.42 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.0 to 2.4.41.

Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.

Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.17, 2.4.18,

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server HTTP/2 SETTINGS Data Processing DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.17 - 2.4.57 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS)
  vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.17 through 2.4.57.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 - 2.4.65 Authentication Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to an authentication bypass
  vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.7 through 2.4.65.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
By sending request bodies in a slow loris way to plain resources, the h2
  stream for that request unnecessarily occupied a server thread cleaning up that incoming data. This affects only
  HTTP/2 connections. A possible mitigation is to not enable the h2 protocol.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.6 - 2.4.46 Tunneling Misconfiguration Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to a tunneling misconfiguration
  vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.6 to 2.4.46 on Linux.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_http2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Using fuzzed network input, the http/2 request handling could be made to
  access freed memory in string comparison when determining the method of a request and thus process the request
  incorrectly.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes
  ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while
  other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.17 < 2.4.64 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service (DoS)
  vulnerability via HTTP/2.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.17 through 2.4.63.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.64 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.63 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.66 SSI Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to a Server Side Includes (SSI)
  vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server prior to version 2.4.66.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.x < 2.4.66 Improper Neutralization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to an improper neutralization
  vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.x prior to 2.4.66.

NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

This script consolidates the OS information detected by several
  VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.

  Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It
  also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.

  If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the
  referenced community forum.

Summary: Nginx is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS:
Nginx versions 1.0.7 and later, 1.1.3 and later.

  Note: The issues only affect nginx if it is built with the ngx_http_mp4_module which is not built
  by default, and the mp4 directive is used in the configuration file.

Summary: nginx is prone to a 1-byte memory overwrite vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS:
nginx version 0.6.18 through 1.20.0.

  Note: The issue only affects nginx if the 'resolver' directive is used in the configuration file.
  Further, the attack is only possible if an attacker is able to forge UDP packets from the DNS
  server.

Summary: nginx is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: nginx up to the most recent version 1.21.1.

Summary: nginx is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: nginx versions 0.5.6 through 1.12.0 and 1.13.x before 1.13.3.

Two security issues were identified in the nginx HTTP/2 implementation,
  which might cause excessive memory consumption and CPU usage.
Affected Software/OS: nginx versions 1.9.5 up to 1.14.0 and 1.15.x up to 1.15.5.

Summary:
nginx is prone to multiple denial of service (DoS)
  vulnerabilities in the HTTP/2 implementation.
Affected Software/OS: nginx versions 1.9.5 through 1.17.2.

NVT Name: nginx 1.1.3 - 1.15.5 Denial of Service and Memory Disclosure via mp4 module
Summary:
A security issue was identified in the ngx_http_mp4_module, which might
  allow an attacker to cause infinite loop in a worker process, cause a worker process crash, or might result
  in worker process memory disclosure by using a specially crafted mp4 file.
Affected Software/OS: nginx versions 1.1.3 through 1.15.5.

Summary:
nginx, with certain error_page configurations, allows HTTP request smuggling,
  as demonstrated by the ability of an attacker to read unauthorized web pages in environments where nginx is
  being fronted by a load balancer.
Affected Software/OS: nginx versions 0.7.12 - 1.17.6.

NVT Name: SSL/TLS: BREACH attack against HTTP compression
Summary:
SSL/TLS connections are vulnerable to the 'BREACH' (Browser
  Reconnaissance & Exfiltration via Adaptive Compression of Hypertext) attack.
Affected Software/OS:
BREACH is a category of vulnerabilities and not a specific
  instance affecting a specific piece of software. To be vulnerable, a web application must:

  - Be served from a server that uses HTTP-level compression

  - Reflect user-input in HTTP response bodies

  - Reflect a secret (such as a CSRF token) in HTTP response bodies

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_http2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Using fuzzed network input, the http/2 request handling could be made to
  access freed memory in string comparison when determining the method of a request and thus process the request
  incorrectly.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
By sending request bodies in a slow loris way to plain resources, the h2
  stream for that request unnecessarily occupied a server thread cleaning up that incoming data. This affects only
  HTTP/2 connections. A possible mitigation is to not enable the h2 protocol.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes
  ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while
  other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

Summary:

  vulnerability.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
By sending request bodies in a slow loris way to plain resources, the h2
  stream for that request unnecessarily occupied a server thread cleaning up that incoming data. This affects only
  HTTP/2 connections. A possible mitigation is to not enable the h2 protocol.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes
  ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while
  other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_http2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Using fuzzed network input, the http/2 request handling could be made to
  access freed memory in string comparison when determining the method of a request and thus process the request
  incorrectly.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Report default community names of the SNMP Agent
Summary:
Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) is a protocol which
  can be used by administrators to remotely manage a computer or network device. There are typically
  2 modes of remote SNMP monitoring. These modes are roughly 'READ' and 'WRITE' (or PUBLIC and
  PRIVATE).

The remote SNMP daemon allows distributed reflection and
  amplification (DRDoS) attacks.

NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

Summary:
The OpenSSH client code between 5.4 and 7.1p1 contains experimental support for resuming SSH-connections (roaming).
  The matching server code has never been shipped, but the client code was enabled by default and could be tricked by a malicious
  server into leaking client memory to the server, including private client user keys. The authentication of the server host key prevents exploitation
  by a man-in-the-middle, so this information leak is restricted to connections to malicious or compromised servers.

NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

This routine uses information collected by other routines about
  CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server, with MPM event, worker or prefork, code executing in
  less-privileged child processes or threads (including scripts executed by an in-process scripting interpreter)
  could execute arbitrary code with the privileges of the parent process (usually root) by manipulating the
  scoreboard. Non-Unix systems are not affected.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to read
  secret data from process memory if the Limit directive can be set in a user's .htaccess
  file, or if httpd.conf has certain misconfigurations, aka Optionsbleed.
Affected Software/OS:
Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x versions up to 2.2.34 and
  2.4.x below 2.4.28.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.17 < 2.4.49 mod_proxy HTTP/2 Request Smuggling Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Apache HTTP Server is prone to an HTTP/2 request smuggling
  vulnerability in the 'mod_proxy' module.
Affected Software/OS:
Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.17 through 2.4.48 running the
  mod_proxy module together with an enabled HTTP/2 protocol.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server mod_session checks the session expiry time before
  decoding the session. This causes session expiry time to be ignored for mod_session_cookie sessions since the
  expiry time is loaded when the session is decoded.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:

  threaded server could allow a user with valid credentials to authenticate using another username, bypassing
  configured access control restrictions.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.6 - 2.4.46 Tunneling Misconfiguration Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:

  vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.6 to 2.4.46 on Linux.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
By sending request bodies in a slow loris way to plain resources, the h2
  stream for that request unnecessarily occupied a server thread cleaning up that incoming data. This affects only
  HTTP/2 connections. A possible mitigation is to not enable the h2 protocol.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes
  ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while
  other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_http2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Using fuzzed network input, the http/2 request handling could be made to
  access freed memory in string comparison when determining the method of a request and thus process the request
  incorrectly.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.17 < 2.4.64 DoS Vulnerability - Linux

  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.

  Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:

  After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that
  a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older
  CPE.

This script consolidates the OS information detected by several
  VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.

  Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It
  also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.

  If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the
  referenced community forum.

The script reports information on how the hostname of the target
  was determined.

Collect information about the network route and

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 Privilege Escalation Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server, with MPM event, worker or prefork, code executing in
  less-privileged child processes or threads (including scripts executed by an in-process scripting interpreter)
  could execute arbitrary code with the privileges of the parent process (usually root) by manipulating the
  scoreboard. Non-Unix systems are not affected.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 mod_session_cookie Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
In Apache HTTP Server mod_session checks the session expiry time before
  decoding the session. This causes session expiry time to be ignored for mod_session_cookie sessions since the
  expiry time is loaded when the session is decoded.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

Summary: Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.2 to 2.4.43.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_auth_digest Access Control Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

Summary:

Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server versions 2.4.6 to 2.4.46 on Linux.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.38 HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
By sending request bodies in a slow loris way to plain resources, the h2
  stream for that request unnecessarily occupied a server thread cleaning up that incoming data. This affects only
  HTTP/2 connections. A possible mitigation is to not enable the h2 protocol.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.37 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 URL Normalization Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
When the path component of a request URL contains multiple consecutive slashes
  ('/'), directives such as LocationMatch and RewriteRule must account for duplicates in regular expressions while
  other aspects of the servers processing will implicitly collapse them.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

NVT Name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.39 mod_http2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability - Linux
Summary:
Using fuzzed network input, the http/2 request handling could be made to
  access freed memory in string comparison when determining the method of a request and thus process the request
  incorrectly.
Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.38 and prior.

Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.63 and prior.

Affected Software/OS: Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.17 through 2.4.63.

NVT Name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
Summary:
The script consolidates and reports various information for web
  application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.

  This information is based on the following scripts / settings:

  - HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)

  - No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

  - Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)

  - Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)

  - The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use

  - The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and
  'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the
  'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use

  If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community
  forum.
